LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SOO KANG AND JONATHAN CHAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN PARAGRAPHS 100, 110, 118, 124, 130 AND NUMBER 6 IN THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH MALICE BY INTENTIONALLY AND DELIBERATELY MAKING NOISE AND EMITTING VIBRATIONS AFTER PROMISING PLAINTIFF THEY WOULD MITIGATE THE NUISANCE. (FAC 64-66, 84.) DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPHS 93 AND 99 ARE DENIED AS PLAINTIFF IS PERMITTED TO RECOVER DAMAGES IN A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM FOR DETRIMENT PROXIMATELY CAUSED BY DEFENDANTS' BREACH. (SEE CAL. CIV. CODE 3300.) DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH 100 IS DENIED AS THE ALLEGATION DOES NOT PRAY FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGE FOR A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S RULING ON DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FAC, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE THE SECOND AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-19-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-15-2013 Set For Trial On Apr-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: mark edson, esq., 510.444.7688, for defendants soo kang and jonathan chan; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SOO KANG and JONATHAN CHAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the second, third and seventh causes of action, and is sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. (See CACI 325.) Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendants breached their duty (FAC 102, 103), and that Plaintiff suffered personal injury damages (FAC 104) and real property damages (FAC 49, 50) as a result of Defendants' alleged conduct. (See CACI 300.) Defendants' demurrer to the seventh cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled, with adequate particularity, the elements of promissory fraud. (See CACI 1902; FAC 126-129.) Defendants' demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot, as a matter of law, show that impact noises and the emission of vibrations are a physical intrusion into Plaintiff's privacy. (See CACI 1800.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: mark edson, esq., 510.444.7688, for defendants soo kang and jonathan chan; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SOO KANG and JONATHAN CHAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Defendant's motion to strike punitive damages in paragraphs 100, 110, 118, 124, 130 and number 6 in the prayer for relief is denied. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendants acted with malice by intentionally and deliberately making noise and emitting vibrations after promising Plaintiff they would mitigate the nuisance. (FAC 64-66, 84.) Defendants' motion to strike paragraphs 93 and 99 are denied as Plaintiff is permitted to recover damages in a breach of contract claim for detriment proximately caused by Defendants' breach. (See Cal. Civ. Code 3300.) Defendants' motion to strike paragraph 100 is denied as the allegation does not pray for punitive damage for a breach of contract claim. In light of the Court's ruling on Defendants' demurrer to the FAC, Defendants' motion to strike the second and seventh causes of action is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
